note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Additive/ApproximateSubgroup.lean:60 — structure IsApproximateSubgroup (K : ℝ) (A : Set G) : Prop (one_mem, inv_eq_self, sq_covBySMul) is exactly Tao's K-approximate-subgroup notion; docstring cites Tointon's 'Introduction to approximate groups'.
verify_note: decl exists (line 63, note's 60 is close enough); docstring 'symmetric set A containing identity s.t. A*A covered by K translates of A' is verbatim Tao's approximate-group definition, Wikidata Q28455592 label 'Approximate group' confirmed — exact is honest.

note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Stieltjes.lean:110 — StieltjesFunction (monotone, right-continuous f) builds the Lebesgue–Stieltjes measure; this is the measure-theoretic relative, not the classical Riemann-sum/partition-tag Riemann–Stieltjes integral (no such construction found under any name).
verify_note: decl exists (line 110 confirmed exact); grepped all Stieltjes-named files in mathlib4 and confirmed no Riemann-sum/tagged-partition R-S integral exists anywhere — proposer's own disclaimer is accurate, and 'related'/low confidence (not 'exact') is the honest, non-overclaiming label.

note: Mathlib/GroupTheory/PGroup.lean:26 — def IsPGroup (p) (G) : Prop := ∀ g : G, ∃ k, g ^ p ^ k = 1, the standard order-is-a-power-of-p definition.
verify_note: decl exists at line 26 exactly; docstring 'A p-group is a group in which the order of every element is a power of p' is verbatim the Wikidata description (Q286972, '𝒑-group') — exact is correct.

note: Mathlib/RepresentationTheory/Basic.lean:49 — abbrev Representation k G V := G →* (V →ₗ[k] V) is the monoid/group-action instance of the general 'representation' concept (Wikipedia's article is broader: Lie algebras, categories, etc. are not covered by this single decl).
verify_note: decl exists at line 49 exactly, abbrev confirmed as G →* (V →ₗ[k] V); Wikidata Q2905654 description 'an object whose endomorphisms are isomorphic to another structure' is genuinely broader than the monoid-hom-specific decl, so special_case (not exact) is the honest, schema-legitimate call per SCHEMA.md's exact/generalization/special_case triad.
